results.title: LVMH FY2025 Results: Revenue €85.7 Billion, Organic Growth Returns in H2
results.snippet: LVMH Moët Hennessy Louis Vuitton reported full-year 2025 revenue of €85.7 billion, down 2% reported but with 3% organic growth in H2, signalling a recovery in luxury demand. Fashion and Leather Goods, led by Louis Vuitton and Christian Dior, contributed €42.2 billion. Wines and Spirits revenue declined 8% as Cognac destocking continued. Operating profit was €19.6 billion; profit attributable to LVMH shareholders was €7.2 billion.

results.title: LVMH Japan and Europe Drive Luxury Recovery as China Normalises
results.snippet: LVMH's recovery in H2 2025 was led by Japan (+23% organic) and Europe (+8%), offsetting a continued slowdown in Mainland China (-7%). CEO Bernard Arnault said the group's diversification across 75 Maisons in six business divisions provides resilience. Selective Retailing (Sephora, DFS, Le Bon Marché) saw revenue of €18.0 billion, up 5%, as beauty consumers remained resilient globally.

results.title: LVMH Invests €3.2 Billion in Capex for Flagship Renovations and Production Expansion
results.snippet: LVMH invested €3.2 billion in capital expenditure in 2025 to renovate flagship stores on Champs-Élysées, Fifth Avenue, and Bond Street, and to expand leather goods manufacturing in France and Italy. Louis Vuitton opened a new atelier in Texas in 2024. The group employs 213,000 people globally, with 70% of production in France.
